name: Timberline
description_short: Timberline Federal Credit Union is a well-capitalized Arkansas-based credit union serving 10,819 members with $82.3M in assets across 3 branch locations.
description_long:
Timberline Federal Credit Union was chartered in 1957 and is headquartered in Crossett, Arkansas. The credit union operates with a mission to serve its membership through traditional financial services. As of Q1 2024, Timberline FCU manages $82.3 million in total assets and maintains a membership base of 10,819 individuals across its service area.

Timberline FCU offers core banking services including checking and savings accounts, auto loans, mortgages, and personal lending products. The credit union maintains 3 branch locations and 3 ATM access points for member convenience. They generate fee income from overdraft fees, ATM fees, and credit card services, collecting $346,524 in such fees during Q1 2024. The institution provides auto loan and mortgage lending with historical trend data available for member review.

Timberline FCU is classified as "well-capitalized" with a net worth ratio of 20.03, significantly above the regulatory threshold of 7.0 required for this designation. This strong capital position demonstrates financial stability relative to peer institutions in the $50M-$100M asset range. The credit union is regulated by the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA) and member deposits are insured through the NCUSIF insurance program.

As of Q1 2024, Timberline FCU reported a net loss of $85,061 for the year-to-date period, despite generating $844,350 in net interest income. Total loan portfolio stands at $52 million with an allowance for credit losses of $611,645. The institution operates with 36 full-time and 1 part-time employee, suggesting limited staffing for its $82M asset base. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Fee-based services (overdraft fees, ATM fees, credit card fees)
Member financial reports and account management
Auto loan trend analysis and reporting
pros: Well-capitalized institution with net worth ratio of 20.03, well above the 7.0 regulatory minimum
Established 67-year history (chartered 1957) indicates long-term stability and community presence
Strong capital reserves of $12.1M equity provide financial security for members
NCUA-insured deposits protect member balances up to $250,000
Multiple service channels with 3 branches and 3 ATMs across service area
Competitive net interest income of $844,350 demonstrates effective lending operations
cons: Reported net loss of $85,061 in Q1 2024 raises questions about operational efficiency
Very limited branch and ATM network (3 each) restricts member accessibility
Small staff (36 FT, 1 PT employee) may limit service capacity and responsiveness
Limited to $52M loan portfolio suggests restricted lending capacity for larger credit needs
Website content does not detail specific loan products, rates, terms, or membership eligibility requirements
best_for: Residents of Crossett, Arkansas and surrounding Cross County area seeking local banking relationships
Credit union members seeking NCUA-insured savings and checking accounts with cooperative ownership
Individuals requiring auto loans and mortgages from a financially stable, well-capitalized institution
Community-focused members prioritizing support of local financial institutions
diagnosis: Timberline FCU is best for members in the Crossett, Arkansas region seeking a financially stable credit union with strong capital reserves and NCUA deposit insurance. The main caveat is the institution's Q1 2024 net loss, limited branch/ATM network, and minimal staffing—factors suggesting operational challenges that prospective members should investigate before joining.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
